display.purpose: Fraunces 400, page heroes and headers. Authority via size + serif, never bold.

heading.purpose: Fraunces 500, card/subsection headers; one step below Title.

label.purpose: DM Sans 500 capitalized, metadata/tags; sparing, not all-caps eyebrows.

shadows.name: shadow-sm
shadows.purpose: Subtle resting lift on a few key surfaces. Coffee-tinted.

shadows.name: shadow-focus-visible
shadows.purpose: Focus ring — warm tinted glow, not a hard outline.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: The canonical Reveal scroll-in curve (opacity + 24px rise, 0.6s, fires once).

motion.name: ease-out-standard
motion.purpose: Default state transitions; exponential ease-out, no bounce.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Collapses all motion to static; film grain drops opacity, marquees stop, Reveal renders without entrance.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The confident default action — roasted-bean fill, cream text.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-foreground); border: none; border-radius: 8px; font: 500 0.875rem/1 "DM Sans", system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary), transparent 10%); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring), transparent 50%); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Bordered cream button that fills with accent on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 8px; font: 500 0.875rem/1 "DM Sans", system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: var(--accent); color: var(--accent-foreground); border-color: var(--accent); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring), transparent 50%); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Warm-paper surface (surface-1). Flat at rest, border tone shifts on hover — no resting shadow.
components.html:
Ratnagiri Estate

Washed Arabica from the BabaBudangiri hills — notes of stone fruit, cocoa, and a long caramel finish.
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; padding: 24px; background: var(--card); color: var(--card-foreground); border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklch, var(--border), transparent 40%); border-radius: 14px; transition: border-color 0.5s ease; } .ds-card:hover { border-color: var(--border); } .ds-card-title { margin: 0; font: 500 1.25rem/1.3 "Fraunces", Georgia, serif; letter-spacing: -0.01em; } .ds-card-body { margin: 0; font: 400 1rem/1.625 "DM Sans", system-ui, sans-serif; color: var(--muted-foreground); }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Hairline-stroke field on cream; calm warm focus glow, not a hard browser outline.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" type="text" placeholder="Search coffees, roasters, origins" />
components.css: .ds-input { width: 280px; padding: 8px 12px; background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); border: 1px solid var(--input); border-radius: 8px; font: 400 0.875rem/1.5 "DM Sans", system-ui, sans-serif; transition: border-color 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring), transparent 50%); }

components.name: Badge
components.description: Quiet pale-wheat pill for metadata and tags.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 10px; background: var(--muted); color: var(--foreground); border-radius: 9999px; font: 500 0.75rem/1.2 "DM Sans", system-ui, sans-serif; }

components.name: Accent Brush-Smear
components.description: The locked brand signature: a coffee brush-smear underlining one phrase per page. Real brush stroke as an alpha mask recolored to the accent token.
components.css: .ds-accent-heading { font: 500 1.875rem/1.15 "Fraunces", Georgia, serif; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: var(--foreground); margin: 0; } .ds-accent { position: relative; font-style: italic; white-space: nowrap; padding: 0 0.08em; isolation: isolate; } .ds-accent::after { content: ""; position: absolute; left: -0.12em; right: -0.12em; bottom: 0.02em; height: 0.6em; z-index: -1; background: var(--accent); -webkit-mask: url("/images/stain-smear.png") center / 100% 100% no-repeat; mask: url("/images/stain-smear.png") center / 100% 100% no-repeat; opacity: 0.6; pointer-events: none; }

narrative.overview: ICB looks and reads like a printed specialist's field guide to Indian specialty coffee: warm paper under the words, expert annotations in the margins, knowledge made beautiful and browsable. The ground is cream, the ink is dark roasted-bean brown, and the one moment of color is a terracotta brush-smear that underlines a single phrase per spread. Fraunces carries every heading; DM Sans does the calm reading work. Depth comes from tonal paper layers, not glass or drop shadows. The personality is refined, confident, and editorial, with a tactile, warm hand. It serves the newcomer and the enthusiast in the same surface, and treats discovery and education as co-equal. It rejects generic SaaS marketing, the hipster coffee cliché, the cold marketplace feel, and corporate sterility.
keyCharacteristics: Cream paper ground, roasted-bean ink, a single terracotta accent.
Fraunces serif headings + DM Sans body — contrast pairing, never two similar sans.
Depth via tonal surfaces (surface-0/1/2), not blur or heavy shadow.
One signature: the <Accent> coffee brush-smear, rationed to one phrase per page.
Coffee-tinted shadows, fine film-grain texture, full light/dark parity.
Editorial rhythm enforced structurally through layout primitives, not by reflex.

rules.name: The One Smear Rule
rules.body: Terracotta is the only accent and it appears on roughly one phrase per page — through the <Accent> brush-smear or a glossary underline. Its rarity is the point.

rules.name: The Ink-Never-Black Rule
rules.body: Text is roasted-brown ink (foreground), never #000. The warmth of the paper system breaks the moment a pure-black glyph lands on it.

rules.name: The Hero-Is-Not-Bold Rule
rules.body: The largest type on the page is Fraunces at weight 400. Size and the serif carry the authority; bold display weight reads as shouting and is forbidden on heroes.

rules.name: The h2 != h3 Rule
rules.body: .text-title and .text-heading must never style the same heading level. The visible step between them is the document's spine.

rules.name: The Flat-Paper Rule
rules.body: Cards are flat at rest, separated by tonal surface steps and a hairline border. Shadow appears only on hover/elevation; reach for surface-1 first.

rules.name: The Warm-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows are coffee-tinted via color-mix toward accent/foreground. A neutral-gray shadow on this cream system is prohibited.

dos: Do keep cream paper as the ground, roasted-bean ink as the text, and terracotta as the only accent.
Do route every section header, padding, and decoration through the layout primitives (Section, PageShell, Stack, Cluster, Rule, Prose, Decor).
Do convey depth with tonal surface steps (surface-0/1/2) and hairline borders; let coffee-tinted shadows appear only on hover/elevation.
Do use Fraunces at weight 400 for heroes — size and serif carry authority, not bold weight.
Do hold the visible step between .text-title (h2) and .text-heading (h3); never skip heading levels.
Do keep body text at AA contrast on both cream and card, in light and dark, including placeholders.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion fallback.
Do ration the <Accent> brush-smear to one phrase per page; keep it in family with .glossary-term.
donts: Don't ship generic SaaS/startup marketing patterns: no hero-metric template, no repeated identical icon-heading-text card grids, no eyebrow above every section, no gradient accents.
Don't drift toward the hipster/third-wave coffee cliché: no kraft-paper, chalkboard, or faux-rustic stamp aesthetic, and no gatekeeping tone.
Don't make it feel like a cold marketplace/Amazon: no dense, price-first product grids without an editorial point of view.
Don't go corporate/sterile: no flat gray, soulless enterprise UI — warmth is non-negotiable.
Don't put a resting drop shadow on a card; reach for surface-1 and a border first.
Don't use a neutral-gray shadow — shadows are coffee-tinted toward accent/foreground.
Don't use pure black for text or #fff outside the on-media scrim variants.
Don't introduce a second accent color, a second radius scale, or a second emphasis language. One accent, one radius, one theme per page.
Don't use a colored left/right border >1px as a card/list/callout/alert accent. Magazine accents live on the top edge (<Decor stripe>), never the side.
Don't use wide-tracked all-caps eyebrows as section scaffolding; labels are capitalized print-UI caps, used sparingly.
